caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing the 2020 coronavirus-related distribution rules for Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs). The page explains that since RMDs were waived in 2020, account owners or beneficiaries who received an RMD had the option to return it to their IRA or other qualified plan to avoid taxes on the distribution. It also mentions that a 2020 RMD qualifying as a coronavirus-related distribution can be repaid over a three-year period or have the taxes due spread over three years. Additionally, the video highlights that withdrawals from an inherited IRA could not be repaid but could be spread over three years for income inclusion. The webpage provides links for more information and directs taxpayers to resources like forms, instructions, publications, and FAQs regarding RMDs available on the IRS.gov website. The cursor is visible, indicating that the user might be navigating or highlighting specific sections of the text.
